issues.description: BANNED guard exempts any user who is also Space.ownerId (`!space && ...`). That leaves a reachable bypass for a BANNED owner: socket join blocks `member.restriction === "BANNED"` unconditionally, and the admin members route only protects OWNER from non-superAdmin actions, so a superAdmin can still create an OWNER+BANNED state. Such a user can then obtain a LiveKit token directly and rejoin media.
issues.recommendation: Make the token route reject `spaceMember?.restriction === "BANNED"` regardless of owner status, or enforce owner-ban impossibility at the mutation boundary and add a regression test for owner+BANNED token denial/immutability.
